item.title: Fantastical 4.1.15 Adds Calendar Mirroring 摘要
item.description:
Flexibits' Fantastical 4.1.15 introduces Calendar Mirroring, allowing users to connect two separate calendars (e.g., work and personal) so events from one automatically appear on the other. No event data is sent to Flexibits servers; it remains on-device. Users can choose to show full details or mark events as 'Busy' to hide specifics. The feature is useful for blocking personal time on work calendars without revealing details. The author uses it to mirror Dithering podcast recording events from a shared Microsoft 365 calendar to a family iCloud calendar, with Fantastical's 'Combine identical events' preventing duplicates. Fantastical also now supports Anthropic's MCP for AI agent integration.
标签: Fantastical, Calendar Mirroring, Flexibits, privacy, calendar, productivity, MCP, AI integration, iCloud, Microsoft 365

item.title: From the DF Archive: ‘Electron and the Decline of Native Apps’ 摘要
item.description:
In 2018, the author expressed concern about Electron's impact on native Mac apps but was not entirely pessimistic. Eight years later, native Mac apps are resurgent, with examples like ChatGPT (AppKit) alongside Electron-based Claude. Apple's Journal app for macOS is disappointing and un-Mac-like, while the new Siri app feels more native. The irony is that Anthropic's Claude, capable of generating high-quality native code, itself uses Electron. The author concludes that the threat from non-native frameworks has not worsened, and native Mac development is thriving.
标签: Electron, native apps, Mac, AppKit, SwiftUI, Claude, ChatGPT, Apple, macOS, software development

item.title: Day One Journal 摘要
item.description:
Day One Journal, a long-standing journaling app for Mac and iOS, sponsors Daring Fireball. The app is praised for its technical and design excellence, respecting the personal nature of journaling. It supports multiple journals and offers a new feature called Daily Chat, an AI-powered guided reflection that helps users structure their thoughts into journal entries. Early testers report it makes journaling effortless and intuitive, transforming the writing experience.
标签: Day One, journaling, app, sponsorship, AI, Daily Chat, iOS, Mac, reflection, productivity

item.title: Better Models: Worse Tools 摘要
item.description:
Armin Ronacher reports that newer Claude models (Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5) sometimes call Pi's edit tool with extra, invented fields in the nested edits array, causing tool call rejection. This issue is absent in older models. He theorizes that Anthropic's reinforcement learning for Claude Code's built-in edit tools may degrade performance on custom tools used by third-party harnesses like Pi. The article questions whether such harnesses should implement multiple edit tools to match the best-performing one for each underlying model.
标签: AI, LLM, tool-use, Anthropic, Claude, coding-agents, Pi, reinforcement-learning, model-regression

item.title: Building a World Map with only 500 bytes 摘要
item.description:
Iwo Kadziela, assisted by Codex, created a credible ASCII world map using only 445 bytes of data. The key technique involves deflate compression, with a JavaScript snippet that uses fetch() on a data: URI, pipes the response through DecompressionStream('deflate-raw'), and renders the decompressed text as a pre element. The approach demonstrates efficient data compression and creative use of browser APIs to generate complex visual output from minimal data.
标签: ASCII art, compression, JavaScript, data URI, world map, creative coding, browser API, deflate, minimalism, hacker news

item.title: What buffer size (OpenSSH) ssh seems to use for streaming output 摘要
item.description:
When piping SSH output (e.g., tar over SSH) to a local file, SSH's write buffer size affects disk I/O patterns. On Linux, SSH typically uses 32 KB or 64 KB writes, not the 128 KB blocks preferred by ZFS. To reblock output, tools like dd (with proper options), buffer, or mbuffer can be used. Tracing SSH's system calls with strace reveals that modern OpenSSH redirects stdout to fd 5, not fd 1. This post documents negative results and practical workarounds for optimizing SSH pipe throughput.
标签: SSH, buffer size, I/O optimization, strace, ZFS, dd, Linux, sysadmin, networking, performance
